latest.title: Mirror Security Launches Encrypted AI Inference
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5729T
Mindflair PLC
19 February 2026
19 February 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Mirror Security
Launches
N
VIDIA
P
owered
E
ncrypted AI
I
nference for
S
ensitive
D
ata
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") and related technology, notes that Mirror Security Limited ("Mirror"), a Dublin-based cybersecurity company specialising in data protection across the generative A
I ecosystem, has announced
full production availability of
its Encrypted AI Inference platform
utilising
NVIDIA
Accelerated Computing
, solving a problem that
has
blocked AI adoption in healthcare, finance, government, and defence.
Mirror is a portfolio com
pany held within
Sure Valley Ventures
'
("SVV")
third fund ("SVV3")
,
in which Mindflair holds an interest
.
Mirror, spun out of University College Dublin, enables AI systems to run inference on encrypted data - processing sensitive information without ever decrypting it. Mirror's platform keeps data encrypted throughout computation.
The platform integrates NVIDI
A AI inference capabilities across NVIDIA's accelerated computing infrastructure,
enabling Secure AI Inference and Secure AI Memory, where data remains encrypted throughout computation and storage
.
Mirror operates across Ireland, the USA and India, working with enterprises, government agencies and defence organisations that require AI deployment without compromising confidentiality or regulatory compliance.
Th
is
launch supports India's ambitious IndiaAI Mission to build comprehensive indigenous AI capabilities. Yotta Data Services, NVIDIA's partner in India and operator of the 16,000+ GPU Shakti Cloud, will be
the
first to deploy Mirror's platform for Indian ente
rprises, government, and defence organi
s
ations.
Mirror believes that this launch supports regulated organisations in deploying AI at scale while maintaining encryption and data control.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
Mirror's
full production launch using
NVIDIA
AI Infrastructure
is a major milestone
for the company
-taking encrypted AI inference into
full
production and enabling regulated organisations to adopt AI at scale without decrypting sensitive data or compromising privac
y and compliance.
"
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

latest.title: Significant progress for Vizgard
latest.content:
13 Feb 2026 10:30
RNS Number : 9628S
Mindflair PLC
13 February 2026
13 February 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Significant progress for Vizgard - portfolio AI drone company
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology, is pleased to provide an update on its portfolio company Vizgard Limited ("Vizgard").
2025 was a successful year for Vizgard (
https://vizgard.com/
), with the company exceeding key milestones across product development and commercial execution. The company achieved significant growth in its SaaS revenues during the year, alongside global deployment of FortifAI and the successful build and launch of UnifAI.
Vizgard is currently participating in NATO's Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic ("DIANA") programme, having been selected from nearly 4,000 applicants, with just 150 companies chosen globally. DIANA is a highly selective six-month accelerator focused on advancing cutting-edge dual-use technologies and includes up to €100,000 of investment.
Alex Kehoe, the founder and CEO of Vizgard explains that participation in DIANA provides Vizgard with a direct route to adoption, enabling NATO organisations to procure its technology via the Rapid Adoption Service, without the need for a separate competitive tender process. This allows Vizgard to accelerate deployment with real, paid end users across allied defence and security environments.
Watch founder and security expert Alex Kehoe dive deeper into the programme and recent wins here:
https://youtu.be/aHSQdnPFa7A?si=81Fu1U7m2BjwLzr_
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

Nicholas Lee, Director

Notes to Editors

The Company is building an investment portfolio of high-tech businesses focused on Artificial Intelligence, across such areas as Internet of Things, Cyber Security, Machine Learning, Immersive Technologies and Big Data, which the Board believes demonstrate evidence of traction and the potential for exponential growth, due to increasing global demand for development in these sectors. For further information, visit: http://www.mindflair.tech/
.
Vizgard is a UK-based AI technology company delivering visual autonomy solutions for security and defence applications. Its platform enables real-time automation and collaboration across cameras and optical systems, supporting defence, law enforcement and critical infrastructure operators. Further information about Vizgard can be found here:
https://vizgard.com/#how-it-works

latest.title: New Investment in AI Sales Execution Company
latest.content:
Today 10:45
RNS Number : 9210Q
Mindflair PLC
29 January 2026
29 January 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
New
I
nvestment in AI
S
ales
E
xecution
C
ompany
Mindflair plc, the
AIM-quoted
company
focused on
investing in
Artificial Intelligence ("AI"),
related
technology is pleased to announce that Sure Valley Ventures
'
("SVV"),
third fund ("SVV3")
in which Mindflair holds an interest, has made a new investment in Overpath Limited ("Overpath"), an Ireland-based AI sales execution platform.
Overpath has raised €1.6 million in pre-seed funding, in a round led by Elkstone Ventures, with participation f
rom SVV and other investors. The funding will be used to accelerate product development, expand enterprise design partnerships and further develop Overpath's execution intelligence platform.
Overpath is building an AI-powered sales execution layer for rev
enue teams, designed to help organisations identify execution risk early in the sales cycle and reinforce the right behaviours while deals are progress
ing
. The platform analyses live qualitative and behavioural signals across sales calls, deal activity, an
d CRM systems, using a domain-specific Sales Language Model trained on real sales behaviours, deal methodologies and execution patterns used by high-performing teams. This enables sales leaders and managers to
identify
execution gaps
during a sales process
and recommend next-best actions within existing workflows
, in turn
moving beyond retrospective reporting
and
towards proactive intervention.
Overpath was founded by Ross Keating and Dermot O'Connor, both experienced operators in enterprise software and go-to-market execution. The company is working closely with early design partners to standardise execution quality, improve coaching effectivene
ss and drive more predictable revenue outcomes.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
This investment in Overpath demonstrates the strategy of backing highly differentiated AI software companies addressing real operational challenges. Overpath is tackling a critical gap in the revenue technology stack by focusing on sales execution rather t
han retrospective analytics, and we believe the company is well positioned to become an important platform in modern enterprise sales
."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

latest.title: Portfolio company selected for NATO alliance
latest.content:
12 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 2258L
Mindflair PLC
12 December 2025
12 December 2025
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Portfolio AI drone company selected for NATO technology alliance
Mindflair plc
, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology,
is pleased to announce that one of its portfolio companies, Vizgard Limited ("Vizgard") (
https://vizgard.com
), has been selected for NATO's prestigious Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic ("DIANA") 2026 cohort.
Vizgard was selected from nearly 4,000 submissions, one of the most competitive defence technology programmes in the world, shaping the future of defence and security technology across NATO alliance nations.
Technology and market positioning
Vizgard is building the UK's sovereign visual autonomy layer - an edge-to-cloud platform stack that enables defence and security systems to think, adapt and collaborate in real time. Its edge-based visual autonomy software automates cameras and optical systems used to protect critical national assets, replacing repetitive and prolonged human monitoring tasks with AI-driven decision making.
The company already works with customers including the UK Ministry of Defence, the Home Office, HMGCC Co-Creation, leading British camera manufacturer Sesanti, and multiple national police departments.
Strategic significance of DIANA participation
As part of the DIANA 2026 cohort, Vizgard will receive:
·
€100,000 of non-dilutive funding
·
Access to more than 180 NATO test centres
·
Expert technical and commercial mentorship
·
A streamlined procurement pathway across NATO alliance nations
These benefits position DIANA as one of the most respected defence innovation programmes globally.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair, commented
:
"
Vizgard's selection for NATO's DIANA programme is a strong endorsement of its technology and strategic relevance within defence and security markets. It clearly supports our conviction of Vizgard's long-term growth potential
."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

latest.title: New Investment in AI Data Security Company
latest.content:
3 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 9550J
Mindflair PLC
03 December 2025
3 December 2025
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
New Investment in AI Data Security company Mirror Security Limited
Mindflair plc,
the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology
, is pleased to announce that Sure Valley Ventures
'
("SVV")
thir
d fu
nd
("S
VV
3"
)
, in which Mindflair holds an interest, has made a new investment in Mirror Security Limited ("Mirror"), a Dublin-based cybersecurity company specialising in data protection across the generative AI ecosystem.
Spun out of University College Dublin (UCD),
Mirror has raised
US
$2.5 million in pre-seed funding in a round co-led by SVV and Atlantic Bridge, with participation from strategic angel investors
, to
scale its breakthrough encryption platform for AI Security.
The company was founded by Pankaj Thapa and
Dr Aditya Narayana K, a
p
rofessor at UCD and global expert in AI security.
Mirror is addressing one of the most urgent problems in enterprise AI adoption - the lack of reliable data confidentiality in model training and inference. As organisations integrate AI into their operations, the risk of exposing proprietary data during AI
processing has surged. Mirror solves this through its revolutionary Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) technology, VectaX, which allows AI systems to process sensitive data while keeping it completely encrypte
d.
On 24 November 2025, Mirror
announced a multi-million-dollar strategic agreement (the "Partnership") with Inception AI ("Inception"), a G42 company and a leading innovator of agentic AI solutions in the UAE. Under the Partnership, Mirror will deploy its full AI security stack across Inception's enterprise and government ecosystem, enabling secure, scalable AI adoption globally. Mirror has also forged strategic partnerships with Intel, MongoDB, Qdrant, SiSys AI & Accops.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
This investment in Mirror highlights
our
continued commitment to
support
pioneering AI software companies that are addressing mission-critical enterprise challenges. Mirror's technology is a true enabler for secure AI adoption, ensuring data privacy while allowing organisations to harness the full potential of AI.
"
The Direct
ors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.
Enquiries:
Mindflair plc
Nicholas Lee, Director
Tel: +44 (0) 20 3368 8961
Nominated Adviser
Cairn Financial Advisers LLP
Liam Murray / Ludovico Lazzaretti
Joint Broker
Shard Capital Partners LLP
Damon Heath
Tel: +44 (0) 20 7213 0880
Tel: +44 (0) 20 7186 9950
Joint Broker
Peterhouse Capital Limited
Duncan Vasey/Lucy Williams
Tel: +44 (0) 20 7469 0935
Tel: +44 (0) 20 7469 0936
Notes to Editors
Mindflair plc (AIM: MFAI) is an investment company providing investors with access to a portfolio of next generation technology businesses focused on AI with significant growth potential. The Company is building an investment portfolio of high-tech businesses focused on Artificial Intelligence, across such areas as Internet of Things, Cyber Security, Machine Learning, Immersive Technologies and Big Data, which the Board believes demonstrate evidence of traction and the potential for exponential growth, due to increasing global demand for development in these sectors. For further information, visit: http://www.mindflair.tech/
.
Mirror Security (www.mirrorsecurity.io) is a cybersecurity company developing encryption technology for the generative AI era. Its flagship platform, VectaX, combines fully homomorphic encryption with vector-level access control to ensure AI systems can process data without ever decrypting it. Founded at University College Dublin, Mirror Security is expanding across Ireland, the US, and India to deliver secure AI infrastructure to global enterprises.

company_30s.what_they_do: MFAI delivers AI‑driven sales execution tools that automate lead qualification, content recommendation and contract drafting for mid‑market firms. Their flagship platform uses encrypted inference engines to keep customer data private while speeding up sales cycles.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global sales‑automation market is moving toward AI‑powered solutions that reduce manual effort and improve conversion rates. MFAI’s focus on privacy‑preserving inference gives it a competitive edge in regulated industries such as finance and healthcare, where data protection is paramount.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in a high‑growth, high‑cash‑burn phase, investing heavily in product development and partner integration. It has not yet posted revenue and is relying on venture funding to reach profitability. The share price is trading near the lower end of its valuation range, reflecting the uncertainty around its business model.

rns_narrative: MFAI is actively building its AI stack, highlighted by the launch of a new encrypted inference engine and a partnership with a major sales‑automation platform. The company has secured contracts with several institutional clients, including a recent DfE award that will drive early adoption.

The company is also expanding its investment portfolio, bringing in new venture partners and exploring cross‑industry collaborations. These moves signal a shift from early‑stage experimentation to a more structured go‑to‑market strategy.
Corporate governance updates – a new board seat for a seasoned AI investor and a change in senior leadership – suggest that MFAI is preparing for a scaling phase.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – investors are watching but not yet vocal.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the AI‑privacy angle and the recent DfE contract as proof that the market is hungry for secure automation tools.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company is burning cash with no clear revenue path and that the AI market is crowded with larger, better‑capitalised competitors.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has remained flat, with a slight uptick after the latest RNS announcing the DfE award. No major negative or positive spikes have occurred.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is essentially dead, giving the stock a high surprise potential if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The first quarterly earnings report – any positive revenue or burn‑rate improvement will validate the business model.
Official announcement of the DfE contract’s commercial phase, which could trigger a volume spike.
Completion of the encrypted inference engine’s beta test with a key partner, potentially leading to a price breakout.
A regulatory approval or data‑privacy certification that boosts investor confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume increase (3‑5× daily average) and a break above the 20‑day moving average. A clear earnings beat or a new partnership announcement will also confirm the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle – capitulation has likely finished, but the mean‑reversion has not yet started. The APEX score suggests a tight compression, so any catalyst could trigger a rapid bounce.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If MFAI’s AI platform gains traction with the DfE contract and the encrypted engine is adopted by a large partner, the stock could rebound from its current low to at least the midpoint of its 52‑week range, delivering a 200‑300% upside. The contrarian model shows that deep crashes (70‑95% down) often produce large rebounds when smart money accumulates.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has no revenue yet and is burning cash. If the earnings report shows no improvement or if the DfE contract stalls, the stock could fall further into the 90‑plus% drop zone, leaving a 70‑80% downside if it never recovers.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200–300% if the mean‑reversion kicks in. Potential downside: 70–80% if the business fails to generate revenue. The risk‑reward ratio is roughly 1:3 to 1:4, but the upside is only realistic if a clear catalyst appears.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 20‑day MA with a 3‑5× volume spike. Size the position to risk no more than 2% of capital per trade, and place a stop 15% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until either the earnings report shows a positive burn‑rate trend or the DfE contract moves into a commercial phase. Also wait for a sustained volume rise of at least 4× the daily average.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. The company has not shown any signs of delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MFAI is a speculative AI play with no revenue yet; it sits at a deep crash but could rebound sharply if the DfE contract and encrypted engine deliver results, offering a high upside against a moderate downside.
